title: Pre-Flight to Touchdown
tagline: One hundred and eighty passengers, a warning light at cruise, and a decision that can't wait for a committee.

wake_up.text: Hotel wake-up call, same as always the night before an early departure. Today's route: six hours, a full aircraft, weather that looks marginal over the second half.

choices.text: Read the full dispatch package before breakfast
Skim it and review the rest at the airport

weather_check.text: The weather brief shows a line of storms building along the back half of the route, timing uncertain. Dispatch has a plan; you'll need to trust it or push back on it.

choices.text: Request extra fuel for weather contingencies
Accept dispatch's fuel plan as filed

walkaround.text: Pre-flight walkaround, rain starting to spit. A small scuff near the left engine cowling catches your eye — probably nothing, definitely worth a second look.

choices.text: Get maintenance to inspect it properly before boarding
Make the call yourself and keep the schedule

boarding.text: One hundred and eighty-two passengers file on. A father with a nervous toddler, a business traveler already asleep, a full flight in every sense.

choices.text: Complete the final checks

taxi.text: Pushback, taxi, a short line for the runway. Everything nominal. Then, rolling toward the threshold, a caution light flickers on the engine display — on, then off.

choices.text: Return to the gate and have it inspected before takeoff
It cleared itself — continue toward takeoff

takeoff_decision.text: Whatever you decided, you're airborne now, climbing out clean through the overcast into clear morning light above the clouds.

choices.text: Settle into the climb

cruise_climb.text: Cruise altitude, autopilot engaged, the cabin settling into the low hum of a normal flight. It stays that way for exactly ninety more minutes.

choices.text: Continue cruise

engine_warning.text: The right engine's oil pressure indicator dips, steadies, dips again. Not a failure — not yet — but not nothing either, and you're ninety minutes from the nearest suitable airport in either direction.

choices.text: Declare an emergency and divert to the nearest airport now
Monitor closely and continue toward the original destination
Work through it together with your first officer before deciding
Reduce power on that engine and proceed cautiously

engine_outcome_divert.text: You're on the ground twenty-five minutes later, well within limits, maintenance already waiting at the gate. A connecting flight gets arranged for the passengers. Nobody remembers this flight by tomorrow, which is exactly the point.

choices.text: Handle the passenger notifications

engine_outcome_monitor.text: The indicator holds steady for the rest of the flight, technically within limits the whole way. It also means ninety minutes of quiet tension in the cockpit that didn't need to happen.

choices.text: Continue toward destination

engine_outcome_consult.text: Between the two of you, the numbers get a second read and a clear call: safe to continue, closely watched, contingency airport identified along the route just in case.

choices.text: Continue with the plan

engine_outcome_reduce.text: Reduced power holds the indicator steady. It costs you some time on the flight plan, but the numbers stay exactly where you want them the rest of the way.

choices.text: Continue toward destination

passenger_medical.text: A flight attendant's voice comes over the interphone: a passenger in the back is having chest pains. There's a doctor on board who's offered to help.

choices.text: Divert to the nearest airport for the passenger
Continue with the onboard doctor monitoring

turbulence_ahead.text: The storm line dispatch flagged this morning is exactly where they said it'd be. The radar shows a path through, but it's tighter than you'd like.

choices.text: Request a longer reroute around the worst of it
Thread the gap the radar shows

descent_prep.text: Descent checklist, approach briefing, the cabin secured. Six hours almost done, one runway left between here and a finished flight.

choices.text: Begin the approach

landing.text: Crosswind on final, gusty but within limits. The wheels touch down exactly where you aimed them.

choices.text: Taxi to the gate

debrief.text: Post-flight paperwork, and a maintenance write-up for anything that came up along the way.

choices.text: Write up every detail from the engine indication onward
Note the essentials and get to the hotel van

end_shift.text: One hundred and eighty-two passengers, on the ground, on time or close to it. Tomorrow's a different route, a different airplane, and the same checklist.

choices.text: Head to the crew van

random_moment.text: Boarding wraps up the way it always does, more or less — a few small, different things happening at the margins today.

choices.text: A kid visiting the cockpit says hi, wide-eyed
Catering runs a few minutes late
Boarding finishes early and clean

triumphant.title: Textbook Flight
triumphant.blurb: Every call was the right one, and it showed. This is the flight your first officer tells other crews about.

steady_hand.title: Safe and Solid
steady_hand.blurb: Nothing dramatic, nothing missed. A hundred and eighty-two people got exactly what they were owed: a boring, uneventful flight.

ordinary_day.title: Routine Sector
ordinary_day.blurb: A normal flight with a couple of interesting moments in the logbook. Tomorrow's sector looks about the same.

burned_out.title: Redlined
burned_out.blurb: You got the aircraft down safely, but you're running on fumes. Fatigue is its own kind of hazard — log it, and rest.

written_up.title: Report Filed
written_up.blurb: A few of today's calls are going into a formal review. Everyone's safe. The company still wants to understand the decisions.
